info.title: pidgeon
info.description: Healthcare data generation, validation, and testing platform. Generate synthetic HL7, FHIR, and NCPDP messages for safe integration testing.

commands.name: catalog
commands.description: Browse, search, and generate from the scenario catalog

commands.name: categories
commands.description: List available scenario categories

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate messages from catalog scenario(s)

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of messages to generate per scenario [default: 1]

options.name: --category
options.description: Generate one message from each scenario in the specified category (ignores scenario-ids)

arguments.name: scenario-ids
arguments.description: One or more scenario IDs to generate messages from

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show detailed information about a scenario

arguments.name: scenario-id
arguments.description: Scenario ID to show details for

commands.name: list
commands.description: List scenarios with optional filters

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search scenarios by term

arguments.name: search-terms
arguments.description: Search terms to match against scenario names, descriptions, and tags

commands.name: completions
commands.description: Generate shell completion setup scripts

commands.name: config
commands.description: Manage configuration intelligence

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Infer vendor patterns from sample messages (auto-detects HL7/FHIR/NCPDP)

options.name: --save
options.description: Save inferred configuration to file (JSON format)

options.name: --samples
options.description: Path to samples (redundant - use positional arg)

arguments.name: samples
arguments.description: Path to file or directory containing sample messages (auto-detects HL7/FHIR/NCPDP)

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new empty configuration profile from template

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name for the new configuration profile

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Delete a configuration profile

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the configuration profile to delete

commands.name: diff
commands.description: Compare two vendor profiles or a profile against a message sample

options.name: --left
options.description: Left vendor name or configuration (e.g., epic)

options.name: --right
options.description: Right vendor name or configuration (e.g., cerner)

options.name: --profile
options.description: Vendor profile to compare against a sample

options.name: --sample
options.description: HL7 message file to compare against a profile

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export pattern as JSON

commands.name: import
commands.description: Analyze sample messages and save as a named configuration profile

options.name: --samples
options.description: Path to sample messages for analysis

commands.name: list
commands.description: List available vendor configurations

commands.name: profile
commands.description: Manage vendor profiles for validation

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a vendor profile from sample messages

options.name: --samples
options.description: Path to sample messages for analysis

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a vendor profile as JSON

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import a vendor profile from JSON

options.name: --file
options.description: JSON file to import as profile

commands.name: list
commands.description: List saved vendor profiles

commands.name: merge
commands.description: Merge two vendor profiles into one

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show detailed profile information

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show detailed configuration information

options.name: --name
options.description: Configuration file or ID to display

commands.name: stats
commands.description: Show configuration catalog statistics

commands.name: telemetry
commands.description: Manage anonymous usage telemetry (opt-in, default: off)

options.name: --show
options.description: Display current telemetry level and what is collected per tier

options.name: --audit
options.description: Display recent telemetry audit log entries

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of audit entries to display (default: 20) [default: 20]

arguments.name: state
arguments.description: Enable or disable anonymous usage telemetry (on|off)

commands.name: use
commands.description: Set active vendor profile for validation

options.name: --name
options.description: Vendor name or configuration file to activate (e.g., epic, cerner, meditech)

commands.name: data
commands.description: Manage clinical terminology data packages

commands.name: extract
commands.description: Run ETL extraction scripts for raw datasets

arguments.name: dataset
arguments.description: Dataset to extract (e.g., loinc, icd10). Omit to extract all.

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import user-supplied data from a file

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to the source data file

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: Data type to import (e.g., cpt)

commands.name: install
commands.description: Install a data package

options.name: --accept-license
options.description: Accept required license terms (e.g., UMLS Metathesaurus License) [default: False]

arguments.name: package
arguments.description: Package name to install

commands.name: list
commands.description: List available and installed data packages

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove an installed data package

arguments.name: package
arguments.description: Package name to remove

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show active data source status and record counts

commands.name: update
commands.description: Check for and download updated vendor intelligence profiles

options.name: --vendor
options.description: Filter updates to a specific vendor name

commands.name: deident
commands.description: De-identify real messages/resources (on-device), preserving referential integrity

options.name: --keep-ids
options.description: Comma-list of identifiers to keep unhashed (e.g., visitId)

options.name: --preview
options.description: Show sample before/after rows without writing files [default: False]

options.name: --in
options.description: File or folder of source data (redundant - use positional args)

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: File or folder of source data

arguments.name: output
arguments.description: Output file/folder (created if missing)

commands.name: flock
commands.description: Generate synthetic patient populations with epidemiological grounding.

commands.name: benchmark
commands.description: Run population generation performance benchmarks.

commands.name: cleanup
commands.description: Remove synthetic data from a target database.

commands.name: compare
commands.description: Show feature comparison matrix vs competitors.

commands.name: connect
commands.description: Connect to a database for schema introspection.

commands.name: demo
commands.description: List and run pre-built demo scenarios.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all available demo scenarios.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run a specific demo scenario.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Demo scenario name

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate a synthetic patient population.

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: sql, csv, hl7, or fhir [default: sql]

options.name: --schema
options.description: Path to SQL DDL or JSON schema

options.name: --state
options.description: State abbreviation for geographic focus (e.g., TX, CA)

options.name: --profile
options.description: Use a learned data profile for generation

options.name: --dir
options.description: Output directory for HL7/FHIR files (Loft integration)

commands.name: learn
commands.description: Extract and learn data patterns from sample records.

commands.name: profile
commands.description: Manage learned data profiles.

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a profile as JSON.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Profile name to export

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import a profile from JSON.

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Path to JSON profile file

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all learned profiles.

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show details of a learned profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Profile name

commands.name: schema
commands.description: View and classify the database schema.

options.name: --classify
options.description: Classify tables by healthcare domain [default: False]

options.name: --visualize
options.description: Output schema as Mermaid ER diagram [default: False]

commands.name: seed
commands.description: Seed synthetic data into a target database.

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show installed Flock data packages.

commands.name: flock-audit
commands.description: Flock compliance audit trails and reporting.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List audit trail entries.

commands.name: report
commands.description: Generate a compliance report for a specific run or workspace.

options.name: --run
options.description: Run ID to generate report for

options.name: --format
options.description: Report format: html, json, csv [default: html]

commands.name: flock-ci
commands.description: Flock CI/CD integration: deterministic generation and verification.

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Run Flock generation in CI mode with deterministic output.

options.name: --ci
options.description: Enable CI mode: deterministic seed, quiet output, JSON results [default: True]

options.name: --schema
options.description: Path to database schema DDL or JSON

options.name: --seed-from-sha
options.description: Git commit SHA to derive deterministic seed

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: sql or csv [default: sql]

options.name: --no-snapshot
options.description: Skip automatic version snapshot after generation [default: False]

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify generated data against a stored manifest.

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate synthetic messages/resources with smart standard inference

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path (optional, defaults to console)

options.name: --vendor
options.description: Apply vendor pattern (e.g., epic, cerner, meditech)

options.name: --hl7-version
options.description: HL7 version for message generation (2.3|2.3.1|2.4|2.5|2.5.1|2.6|2.7|2.8) or 'list' to show available [default: 2.3]

options.name: --no-session
options.description: Force pure random generation (ignore current session) [default: False]

arguments.name: message-types
arguments.description: Message type (e.g., ADT^A01, Patient, NewRx) - supports 239+ HL7/FHIR/NCPDP types

commands.name: golden
commands.description: Clinical acceptance testing suite

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show details of a specific scenario pack

options.name: --pack
options.description: Pack name or directory path to show details for

commands.name: packs
commands.description: List available scenario packs

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run all clinical acceptance scenarios from a directory or pack

options.name: --scenarios
options.description: Path to scenario YAML files directory [default: golden/scenarios/]

options.name: --report
options.description: Path to write the report output (markdown or HTML based on extension)

options.name: --pack
options.description: Run a specific scenario pack by name or directory path (e.g., 'core-clinical')

commands.name: healing
commands.description: Manage self-healing rules, review queue, and circuit breakers.

commands.name: approve
commands.description: Approve a proposed healing rule.

arguments.name: rule-id
arguments.description: The ID of the healing rule to approve

commands.name: history
commands.description: Show audit trail for healing operations.

commands.name: queue
commands.description: Show the review queue of proposed healing rules.

commands.name: reject
commands.description: Reject a proposed healing rule.

arguments.name: rule-id
arguments.description: The ID of the healing rule to reject

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show active healing rules and circuit breaker states.

commands.name: intelligence
commands.description: Manage vendor intelligence network: telemetry, profiles, impact reports, consent.

commands.name: accuracy
commands.description: Show validation accuracy scores by vendor.

commands.name: alerts
commands.description: Show proactive deviation alerts from the network.

commands.name: consent
commands.description: Manage intelligence telemetry consent settings.

commands.name: delete-data
commands.description: Request deletion of all contributed telemetry data (right to be forgotten).

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export local telemetry buffer for air-gapped environments.

commands.name: impact
commands.description: List upgrade impact reports or show specific report.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: Impact report ID for detail view

commands.name: profiles
commands.description: List available crowd-sourced vendor profiles or show detail for a vendor.

arguments.name: vendor
arguments.description: Vendor name for detailed profile view

commands.name: status
commands.description: Display telemetry pipeline status, consent level, and outbox depth.

commands.name: loft
commands.description: Observe and monitor live HL7 interface traffic with real-time validation and alerting.

commands.name: alerts
commands.description: Display recent alert history from the Loft metrics store.

options.name: --since
options.description: Time window to display (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d). Defaults to 1h. [default: 1h]

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity: critical, warning, info, error

commands.name: baselines
commands.description: Show baseline status for monitored interfaces.

options.name: --interface
options.description: Show baseline for a specific interface ID

commands.name: compliance
commands.description: Generate and manage compliance reports.

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: pdf, excel, html. Defaults to pdf. [default: pdf]

commands.name: deviations
commands.description: List active deviations detected by Loft intelligence.

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity: critical, warning, info

options.name: --status
options.description: Filter by status: open, closed, suppressed (default: open)

commands.name: detail
commands.description: Show full detail for a specific deviation.

arguments.name: fingerprint-key
arguments.description: The fingerprint key to inspect.

commands.description: Manage self-healing rules, review queue, and circuit breakers.

commands.description: Manage intelligence telemetry consent settings.

commands.description: List upgrade impact reports or show specific report.

commands.name: partners
commands.description: List trading partners with health status.

commands.name: report
commands.description: Generate a summary report of Loft monitoring activity.

options.name: --since
options.description: Time window for report (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d). Defaults to 24h. [default: 24h]

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, pdf, excel, html. Defaults to text. [default: text]

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate a report in PDF, Excel, or HTML format.

options.name: --template
options.description: Report template: interface-health, partner-scorecard, sla-compliance [default: interface-health]

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: pdf, excel, html. Defaults to pdf. [default: pdf]

commands.name: history
commands.description: Show run history for a schedule.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all report schedules.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Trigger immediate generation of a named schedule.

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create a scheduled report.

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: pdf, excel, html [default: pdf]

commands.name: rules
commands.description: List alert rules with status.

commands.name: reload
commands.description: Reload alert rules from YAML configuration.

commands.name: sources
commands.description: List message sources with health status.

commands.name: reload
commands.description: Reload source configuration from YAML.

commands.name: status
commands.description: Display current Loft monitoring metrics.

commands.name: validate-config
commands.description: Validate YAML configuration files without starting the watcher.

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Watch a directory for incoming .hl7 files and validate them in real time.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Vendor profile name for validation (e.g., epic, cerner)

commands.name: lookup
commands.description: Look up healthcare standards definitions with smart inference

options.name: --search
options.description: Search across all standards for elements matching query

options.name: --segments
options.description: List all segments/resources in specified standard [default: False]

options.name: --children
options.description: List child elements of specified path [default: False]

options.name: --examples
options.description: Show detailed examples and usage patterns [default: False]

options.name: --cross-ref
options.description: Show cross-references to other standards [default: False]

options.name: --inter
options.description: ⚠️  Beta: Interactive TUI browsing mode (in development) [default: False]

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Element path (e.g., PID.3.5, Patient.identifier, NewRx.Patient) - leave empty to browse

commands.name: path
commands.description: Discover and explore semantic field paths across healthcare standards

commands.name: list
commands.description: Discover available semantic paths for a message type

options.name: --examples
options.description: Show example values for each path [default: False]

arguments.name: message-type
arguments.description: HL7 message (ADT^A01), FHIR resource (Patient), or NCPDP type (NewRx). If omitted, shows universal paths.

commands.name: resolve
commands.description: Show how a semantic path maps to standard-specific field locations

options.name: --standard
options.description: Show mapping for specific standard only

options.name: --all-standards
options.description: Show mappings across all supported standards [default: False]

options.name: --detailed
options.description: Include field type, validation rules, and examples [default: False]

options.name: --path-only
options.description: Output only the resolved path (useful for scripting) [default: False]

arguments.name: semantic-path
arguments.description: Semantic path to resolve (e.g., patient.mrn, encounter.location)

arguments.name: message-type
arguments.description: Target message type (ADT^A01, Patient, NewRx)

commands.name: search
commands.description: Find semantic paths by keyword or description

options.name: --message-type
options.description: Limit search to specific message type

options.name: --exact
options.description: Exact match only (no fuzzy search) [default: False]

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: Search term (e.g., "phone", "date", "medical record")

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Check if a semantic path is valid for a given message type

options.name: --suggestions
options.description: Show related/alternative paths when invalid [default: True]

arguments.name: semantic-path
arguments.description: Path to validate (e.g., medication.dosage)

arguments.name: message-type
arguments.description: Target message type (ADT^A01, Patient, etc.)

commands.name: search
commands.description: Simulate FHIR search queries with realistic server responses

options.name: --include
options.description: Include related resources (doctors, encounters, observations, everything)

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path (optional, defaults to console)

arguments.name: resource-type
arguments.description: FHIR resource type to search (Patient, Observation, Encounter, Practitioner, etc.)

commands.name: send
commands.description: Generate and deliver messages to a target path or endpoint

options.name: --to
options.description: Delivery target path or sftp:// URI (fallback for positional arg)

options.name: --scenario
options.description: Scenario ID(s) from the catalog to generate and deliver

options.name: --category
options.description: Deliver all scenarios in a category

options.name: --vendor
options.description: Vendor profile to apply (e.g., epic, centricity/pharmacy_outbound)

options.name: --retries
options.description: Max retry attempts for failed deliveries [default: 3]

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Delivery target path or sftp:// URI

commands.name: session
commands.description: Manage sessions for maintaining field values across commands

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Clear current session

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create new named session and switch to it

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name for the new session

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export session as template

options.name: --session
options.description: Session to export (current if not specified)

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Output file path (JSON format)

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import session template

options.name: --name
options.description: Name for imported session (derived from file if not specified)

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Template file path (JSON format)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all sessions with status

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove specific session

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the session to remove

commands.name: save
commands.description: Save temporary session with a permanent name

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name for the permanent session

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show session details

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of session to show (current session if not specified)

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show current session status

commands.name: use
commands.description: Switch to existing session

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the session to switch to

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set field values with smart session management

options.name: --list
options.description: List current values in the current session [default: False]

options.name: --remove
options.description: Remove the specified field value from the current session [default: False]

options.name: --session
options.description: Override session (advanced: use specific session instead of current)

arguments.name: field
arguments.description: Field path to set (e.g., patient.mrn, PID.3.1, Patient.identifier[0].value)

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to set for the field (optional for --list and --remove operations)

commands.name: upgrade
commands.description: Show subscription status and upgrade options

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validate healthcare messages against standards

options.name: --standard
options.description: Specific standard to validate against (auto-detect if not specified)

options.name: --profile
options.description: Vendor profile name to validate against

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to file (redundant - use positional args instead)

arguments.name: files
arguments.description: Path(s) to message file(s) to validate (supports wildcards)

commands.name: vendor
commands.description: Browse and inspect curated vendor interface profiles

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show details of a specific vendor profile

arguments.name: vendor
arguments.description: Vendor name or profile key (e.g., 'centricity' or 'centricity/pharmacy_outbound')

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all available vendor interface profiles

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Suppress status output (only emit data to stdout)
